title: The future of ZenOS
content:
## so, where's ZenOS going? 

 ...is a question i can imagine you guys asking. there haven't been any pushes to the main branch of zenos in a while and a half, after all... 

 well, i am in fact cooking something up on the experimental branch of zenpkgs and [the new zenos config](https://github.com/zenos-n/zenos-config). 

 ### so, what are you cooking up, doromiert? 

 i am actually working on a DSL for ZenOS, actually, more like 3 DSLs: 
 1. `.zcfg`: the configuration format the end user will interact with 
 2. `.zmdl`: the module format most maintainers will interact with 
 3. `.zstr`: the configuration of the entire ZenPkgs config structure 

 ### why are you doing allat? 

 simple answer: nix by default doesn't provide the UX and DX i'm looking for. 

 i want to be able to build a zenpkgs module in minutes rather than hours. 

 ### how do these fix that? 

 there are some parts to it let's say. 

 first of all, when it comes to zcfg, it basically does 90% of what normal nixos configs used to do but it doesn't require the annoying `{...}: {}` function declaration (which if you're just configuring your OS, not developing something, should be unnecessary). 

 now, when it comes to zmdl, they're designed to use the file structure of the project for the path. for example, `./modules/desktops/gnome.zmdl` will auto attach to desktops.gnome with its children. second of all, it doesn't require you to use the function declarations i hate so much. third of all, it has a pretty neat option tree structure where every arbitrary option is instantly an option without any `mkOption` and you define metadata and more using... 4: keywords. these are pretty unique to how nix usually handles things. (at least as of right now), you have `_meta` for metadata, `_action`, with a `!` shorthand (`_u/saction` for program modules) for what happens when a boolean is true and `_let` for... 5: `$variables`. since i don't want to confuse the compiler (and user) with having to decipher if something is a nix value or a zmdl value, zmdl-specific vars are prefixed with `$`. the builtins are `$type`, `$c` for colors, `$name` for the module name, `$path` for the module path, `$lib` for nixpkgs lib, `$v` for custom `_let` variables, `$pkgs` for ZenPkgs packages, `$l` for licenses, `$m` for maintainers and `$f` for calling freeform names. 

 yes, that's quite a bit but there's way more. i'll make documentation once i'm done with implementing it. 

 oh and i already made a vsc extension for it. 

 that's all i wanted to say: ZenOS isn't dead yet!
